description: Unsolved problems in computational complexity, algorithms, and computability.

facts.context: NP-intermediate problems (in NP but neither in P nor NP-complete) exist if:
facts.truth: P ≠ NP holds (Ladner 1975); factoring and graph isomorphism are candidates
distractors: P = NP holds, in which case all NP problems collapse to polynomial time
the polynomial hierarchy collapses to the second level unconditionally
no such intermediate problems can exist regardless of P vs NP status

facts.context: Graph isomorphism problem's complexity status is:
facts.truth: in quasipolynomial time (Babai 2015); not in P or NP-complete as far as known
distractors: proven NP-complete by reduction from subgraph isomorphism (Garey-Johnson)
solved in polynomial time by Weisfeiler-Leman refinement with bounded color
proven undecidable by reduction from the halting problem for Turing machines

facts.context: The Unique Games Conjecture (Khot 2002) states that:
facts.truth: approximating unique label cover is NP-hard; implies tight inapproximability
distractors: unique games can be solved exactly in randomized polynomial time always
the conjecture was proven true via PCP theorem extension by Dinur (2007)
the conjecture was disproven by a subexponential algorithm found in 2010

facts.context: The relationship BPP =? P concerns whether:
facts.truth: randomized polynomial time equals deterministic polynomial time (believed, open)
distractors: BPP strictly contains P, proven via existence of problems needing randomness
randomness provably adds power beyond P by explicit oracle separation proof
the question is formally independent of ZFC by Razborov-Rudich natural proofs

facts.context: Whether NP = coNP is unknown. If NP ≠ coNP, then:
facts.truth: P ≠ NP follows (contrapositive: P = NP would imply NP = coNP)
distractors: P = NP follows, since coNP separation forces polynomial-time equivalence
no relationship to P vs NP exists; the two questions are fully independent
NP = coNP is already proven true via Immerman-Szelepcsényi for all classes

facts.context: The L vs NL problem asks whether:
facts.truth: nondeterministic log-space equals deterministic log-space (open; NL ⊆ P known)
distractors: L = NL was proven by Reingold's undirected connectivity algorithm in 2005
NL is not contained in P, which separates it from deterministic computation
log-space computation is uncomputable because of the space hierarchy theorem

facts.context: The optimal exponent ω for matrix multiplication satisfies:
facts.truth: 2 ≤ ω ≤ 2.373; conjectured ω = 2, best bound by Williams et al.
distractors: ω = 3 exactly, meaning the naive O(n³) algorithm is provably optimal
ω = 2 is already proven via the laser method of Coppersmith-Winograd
ω > 3 because sub-cubic algorithms violate information-theoretic bounds

facts.context: The information-theoretic lower bound for comparison sorting n elements is:
facts.truth: Ω(n log n) comparisons; matched by mergesort and heapsort algorithms
distractors: Ω(n²) comparisons; no comparison sort can beat quadratic in worst case
O(n) comparisons are achievable via comparison-based distribution sorting
no lower bound exists because sorting is not in any standard complexity class

facts.context: The 3SUM problem (find a+b+c=0 in array) has conjectured complexity:
facts.truth: Ω(n²) in comparison model; recent results show n^(2−o(1)) is tight
distractors: O(n log n) is achievable via reduction to sorting plus binary search scan
3SUM is NP-complete by reduction from subset sum on general integer inputs
Ω(n³) is proven optimal via an adversary argument in the comparison model

facts.context: The Exponential Time Hypothesis (ETH) states that:
facts.truth: 3-SAT requires 2^(Ω(n)) time; implies many conditional lower bounds
distractors: 3-SAT is solvable in polynomial time by advanced DPLL with clause learning
ETH has been formally proven true via circuit complexity barrier arguments
ETH was disproven by Lokshtanov's subexponential parameterized algorithm

facts.context: The complexity of integer factoring is:
facts.truth: in NP ∩ coNP, not known in P or NP-complete; quantum P via Shor
distractors: proven NP-complete by Cook-Levin reduction from Boolean satisfiability (SAT)
known to be in P via the general number field sieve running in polynomial time
proven undecidable by reduction from Hilbert's tenth problem on Diophantine sets

facts.context: The log-rank conjecture in communication complexity states:
facts.truth: deterministic communication ≤ (log rank)^O(1) for boolean matrices (open)
distractors: the conjecture was proven true by Lovett's breakthrough rank bound in 2016
the conjecture was proven false by Chattopadhyay-Lovett counterexample (2019)
deterministic communication exactly equals the logarithm of matrix rank always
